compiled_state.title: As a developer, my CI pipeline blocks PRs with security findings using Semgrep instead of Bandit
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Replace the Bandit security scan step in examples/ci.example.yml with a Semgrep scan using the semgrep/semgrep Docker container, including SARIF output with jq post-processing for GitHub Security tab compatibility
**Why**: Bandit only covers Python; Semgrep covers 30+ languages in a single scan, providing unified SAST coverage across all project stacks
**Scope**:
- IN: Replace Bandit step in ci.example.yml with Semgrep, pinned Docker tag, --metrics=off, --error flag, --severity flag (configurable via semgrep.fail_on), SARIF output to semgrep-results.sarif, jq post-processing to convert string severity to CVSS numerics (workaround for semgrep/semgrep#10834), upload-sarif with continue-on-error: true and if: always(), remove Bandit installation step, set SEMGREP_SEND_METRICS=off env var as defense-in-depth alongside --metrics=off flag
- OUT: Pre-commit hooks for Semgrep, integration with security review overlay, Semgrep Pro features, CodeQL complement

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the CI pipeline template runs semgrep scan with a pinned Docker tag, --metrics=off, and --error, producing SARIF output that is uploaded as a GitHub Actions artifact
  <- Satisfies: "The CI pipeline template runs Semgrep OSS instead of Bandit, using a pinned semgrep/semgrep Docker container tag with --metrics=off and --error"
- When this story is complete, the CI step exits non-zero when Semgrep reports findings at ERROR severity (default) or WARNING severity (when semgrep.fail_on=warning), blocking the PR via required status check
  <- Satisfies: "The CI step runs semgrep scan --error --severity ERROR, causing exit code 1 when findings exist at or above the configured threshold"
- When this story is complete, SARIF upload to GitHub Security tab includes a jq post-processing step and uses continue-on-error: true, decoupled from the blocking gate
  <- Satisfies: "SARIF upload is best-effort and decoupled from the blocking CI gate"
- When this story is complete, Bandit installation and scan steps are removed from the CI template
  <- Satisfies: "runs Semgrep OSS instead of Bandit"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Security] Set SEMGREP_SEND_METRICS=off as env var in CI step alongside --metrics=off flag to defend against env var override
- [Reliability] SARIF jq post-processing — verify jq is available in the semgrep/semgrep Docker container; if not, install it or use Python fallback
- [Testing] CI template changes are hard to test locally — consider YAML linting and structural validation tests
- [Maintainability] Clean up all Bandit references across the codebase (15 files reference bandit/semgrep)

comments.body: Done definition update: The pinned semgrep/semgrep Docker image must be verified to include jq for SARIF post-processing. If jq is not present, use a Python one-liner as fallback (python3 is available in the image). The done definition requires a successful dry-run of the SARIF post-processing step.
comments.author: Claude
